---
title: "UIAO-core Value Proposition"
subtitle: "Two-Way Governance Architecture for SCuBA and BOD 25-01"
author: "Michael Stratton"
date: "2026-04-11"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: false
    theme: cosmo
categories: [governance, scuba, bod-25-01, fedramp]
---

# 1. Executive Summary

Binding Operational Directive 25-01 mandates automated, continuous cloud security assessments across all FCEB agencies. CISA's SCuBA program provides assessment logic through ScubaGear and ScubaGoggles. ScubaConnect automates execution. But neither provides governance. They produce data, not meaning.

UIAO-core fills the missing half as a **governance envelope** -- a two-way prompt-and-conversion mediator. **Outbound (Leg 1)**: captures assessment results, normalizes into canonical artifacts, detects drift, routes violations. **Inbound (Leg 2)**: monitors CISA policy releases, detects new baselines, triggers recalibration.

**SCuBA assesses. ScubaConnect automates. UIAO governs.**

# 2. The Problem: Why SCuBA Alone Cannot Satisfy BOD 25-01

BOD 25-01 requires automated, non-interactive, continuously running assessment tools feeding results directly to CISA. The legacy manual model has five critical weaknesses: human-dependent triggers, interactive authentication, local execution, quarterly frequency, and manual reporting.

# 3. The Solution: UIAO-core as the Governance Envelope

UIAO-core wraps around the entire SCuBA data path -- not inline -- as a governance envelope operating on two legs simultaneously: Prompt (event-driven triggers) and Conversion (raw data to canonical governance artifacts).

## 3.1 Canonical Artifact Repository

The Canonical Artifact Repository is a Git directory structure inside the agency's governance repository -- not a separate database, not a SaaS platform, not a dedicated server.

# 4. Leg 1 -- Outbound: Assessment to Governance to Reporting

Step-by-step data flow from GitHub Actions trigger through ScubaConnect execution, parallel capture by UIAO, normalization to canonical artifacts, drift detection, and dashboard routing.

# 5. Leg 2 -- Inbound: Policy Updates to Governance to Recalibration

UIAO monitors cisagov/ScubaGear for upstream policy changes via GitHub API polling, release tag monitoring, and Rego rule diffing. Detected changes generate governance events that trigger assessment recalibration.

# 6. The Six Value Pillars

1. Continuous Compliance Monitoring
2. Zero-Touch Execution Pipeline
3. Closed-Loop Feedback Architecture
4. Drift-Resistant Canonical Repository
5. Agency-Side Visibility and Accountability
6. Future-Proof Modular Architecture

# 7. Cadence and Latency Profile

Pipeline timing from ScubaConnect execution (45-90 min) through UIAO processing (5-15 min) to dashboard update (near real-time). Three cadence tiers: Daily assessment, Weekly governance review, Event-driven policy absorption.

# 8. The UIAO Agency Dashboard

The Agency Dashboard is NOT CDM. It is a local, agency-owned, Git-rendered governance surface. Implementation tiers: Tier 1 (Markdown/GitHub Pages), Tier 2 (Static site generator), Tier 3 (Full web application).

# 9. Deployment Architecture and Cost Model -- The Hybrid Approach

Two layers: Git server (on-premises, deterministic, air-gappable) and GitHub (cloud-native orchestration, Actions, CI/CD). Git is not GitHub. The hybrid model provides compliance-aligned, cost-effective deployment.

# 10. Quality Assurance and Confidence Profile

Five-segment quality chain from CISA baselines through ScubaGear execution, UIAO normalization, drift detection, to dashboard rendering. Guarantees and non-guarantees explicitly stated.

# 11. Infrastructure Cost and Compliance Profile

GitHub pricing tiers (Free, Team, Enterprise), storage models, FedRAMP postures (GitHub.com vs GHE Server vs GHES on Azure Gov), and deployment implications for GCC-Moderate environments.

# 12. Positioning Statement

UIAO-core is the governance orchestration layer above CISA ScubaGear -- complementary, not competitive. It transforms SCuBA from a compliance assessment tool into a continuously governed, audit-ready compliance pipeline.

# 13. Audience-Ready Formats

Executive summary, pitch-deck slide, GitHub README, and Federal CIO/CISO narrative formats -- all derived from the canonical value proposition.

# Appendix A: PlantUML Diagram Source Reference {.appendix}

PlantUML source for the two-way governance architecture diagram is maintained as a companion artifact: `uiao-scuba-two-way-governance.puml`.

---

*Note: This is the condensed outline version. The full 60KB document with all tables, image placeholders, detailed analysis, and complete narrative is available in the conversation artifact and Outlook draft.*
